description:
Practical backlog summary anchor: **Tier 1 → Temporal reasoning overhaul: preserve the original phrase, but normalize stale relative references to absolute/ranged anchors**.

Backlog detail: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 1. Temporal reasoning overhaul**.

Implementation context:
- current state has only coarse timestamps/cursors in `packages/pi-om-extension/src/types.ts`
- persistence/validation lives in `src/state.ts`
- observation assembly and durable rendering flow through `src/engine.ts`, `src/format.ts`, and `src/prompts.ts`
- this package currently stores date-grouped human-readable observations, so any richer temporal model must still render cleanly in that format

What this epic should establish:
- preserved original time phrasing plus normalized absolute/ranged anchors where confidence is high
- ambiguity handling without fake precision
- better chronology over multi-day sessions, pauses, resumed work, and future planned changes

Useful references:
- backlog doc section 1 plus linked Mastra refs (`observer-agent.ts`, `date-utils.ts`, `observational-memory.mdx`)
- existing local tests in `src/__tests__`, especially state/engine/format coverage

Honor the previously-agreed preference for concrete temporal anchors like `tomorrow (target: YYYY-MM-DD)` and `last week (week of YYYY-MM-DD)` when appropriate.
title: Temporal reasoning overhaul for observational memory
